methods.description: Process for capturing photographs of anatomical specimens and creating textured 3D models using photogrammetry.

steps.description: Photographs of the cranium were taken using a Nikon D5200 camera, equipped with a Sigma 50 mm f/2.8 macro lens, whereas photographs of the ear bones were taken with a Nikon D850 camera equipped with a Nikon Micro Nikkor AF-S 60 mm f/2.8 G ED macro-lens.
steps.searchable_summary: Capture photographs of anatomical specimens using specified camera equipment
inputs: Nikon Micro Nikkor AF-S 60 mm f/2.8 G ED macro-lens

steps.description: Textured 3D models of the cranium and ear bones were elaborated in the Agisoft Metashape software 1.7.6, masking and aligning 37 photographs for the cranium, 71 for the periotic, 78 for the bulla and 103 for the malleus.
steps.searchable_summary: Create textured 3D models from photographs using Agisoft Metashape

steps.description: These photogrammetric models were scaled in Blender 3.5 to their natural size.
steps.searchable_summary: Scale 3D models to natural size using Blender

methods.description: Procedure for preparing and analyzing a smear slide to determine the geological age of a specimen using calcareous nannofossils.

steps.description: A smear slide was prepared from the powder extracted from the sedimentary matrix entombing the studied specimen by using a millimeter tungsten carbide spherical drill bit mounted on an electric drill.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare smear slide from sedimentary matrix using drill bit

steps.description: A calcareous nannofossil assemblage analysis of the smear slide was then performed to determine the geological age of the specimen.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform nannofossil assemblage analysis to determine geological age

steps.description: The smear slide was examined under a light microscope at 1250× magnification.
steps.searchable_summary: Examine smear slide under microscope at 1250× magnification

steps.description: The presence or absence of the main taxa was checked in a prefixed area of approximately 6-7 mm² (roughly equivalent to 3 vertical traverses; modified after Gardin and Monechi [12]).
steps.searchable_summary: Check presence/absence of main taxa in specified area

steps.description: Following Rio et al. [13], the presence/absence of index species was estimated by quantitative counting relative to a predetermined number of taxonomically related forms (i.e., species of the genus Helicosphaera vs. 50 helicoliths; species of the genus Sphenolithus vs. 30 sphenoliths).
steps.searchable_summary: Estimate presence/absence of index species by quantitative counting
